impact:
T-013 ran in a measurement window of ~35 min post-T-014 deploy. Natural traffic in the window consisted entirely of background htmx banner polls (all sub-100ms p95 — no findings). Synthetic unauthenticated probes from inside the container hit only 302 redirects (~1-3ms) plus one false signal (/auth/login at 329ms client-side, which is just urllib following the 302 to EVE SSO HTTPS — vigilant.perf middleware shows the actual handler is 1-2ms).

Heavy authenticated routes were NOT exercised in this session: /admin (the full status page with the new memory gauge), /killmails (list with 405k rows), /fitting (with a fit loaded), /map (with full data load), /activity (dashboard charts), /structures/timers. These are the routes most likely to surface real latency issues.

Prior-container log mining returned nothing useful — vigilant's previous container directories had already been cleaned up by docker; only logs from sibling containers (wanderer-kills) remained on disk.

What was confirmed positive:
- CPU cap is 2.0 (was 0.5 in old memory) — already addressed
- Memory cap is 2.5GB
- Killmail ESI pacing: 3 fetches per 1.5s wave + page sleep 5s = 2 req/s avg (killmail_ingest.py:39-41)
- Disk-first skip: candidates filtered against existing Killmail PKs before fetch (killmail_ingest.py:171-182)
- `now` name shadowing: 4 matches in templates but all are JS-scope (`const now = new Date()`), not Jinja
- cache_stats SELECT COUNT(*) on /admin only counts small operational tables (esi_cache 4k, etc), NOT the big ones (killmails 405k, killmail_attackers 2.5M, wallet_snapshots 748k)

Suggested follow-up (NOT in this ticket's scope): a dedicated session where the user browses the app for ~10 minutes while logs accumulate, or persisting vigilant.perf lines to a rolling file (not just stdout) so a future rebaseline survives container recreates.
resolution:
Resolved in two parts:

(1) Persistence half — commit f5f9296: vigilant.perf logger now also writes to /data/logs/perf.log via RotatingFileHandler (5 MiB × 4 backups). Measurements survive container recreates. Stdout still gets every line. Wired via VIGILANT_PERF_FILE in docker-compose.yml.

(2) Measurement half — user browsed /admin, /tools/fitting, /map, /dashboard, character detail, /tools/activity, /industry. Baseline captured in /data/logs/perf.log. Findings: 7 routes exceed 500ms p95; /tools/activity peaked at 6.2s, /dashboard/corp-stats and /tools/fitting/saved both ~2s. Detailed table filed as a follow-up issue.

Important caveats: sample size n=1-5 per route from a single session, so individual numbers are noisy. The persistence fix means future sessions can accumulate data without losing it.
title: Perf rebaseline incomplete — heavy authed routes not measured this session
